context.setting: Watching TV with TV in Front

episode_text:
day=5; meal=bite-sized pizza and broccoli; setting=Watching TV with TV in Front; time_of_day=morning;
preferences: microwave_time=1 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize front occlusion; robot_speed=fast; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=perception; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=10 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., watching TV on the left or working on a laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, or dessert items with sauce THEN 3 min BECAUSE in both observed left-attention meals the user explicitly chose hotter food. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front (e.g., watching TV in front) AND affective state is neutral AND food has no sauce or dip THEN 1 min BECAUSE repeated observed front-TV meals matched the quiet-meal default rather than showing a need for extra heating.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a social partner or visual focus in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special sauce/dip condition THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE repeated forward-attention meals show the user prefers preserving the forward view. IF dining context includes a TV, laptop, or other visual focus on the left AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, or dessert items with sauce THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE across observed left-attention meals the user explicitly chose to preserve the left-side view.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., TV on the left or laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, or dessert items with sauce THEN slower than default BECAUSE the user selected medium in one left-attention meal and slow in another, indicating a consistent preference to reduce motion intensity when attention is shared with a left-side activity and the food may be messier. IF dining context is working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce or dip THEN slow BECAUSE the user explicitly chose the slowest speed in that observed context. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front (e.g., watching TV in front) AND affective state is neutral AND food has no sauce or dip THEN fast BECAUSE repeated front-TV meals retained the fastest setting.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND feeding transfer occurs THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected this option across observed meals, favoring reliable bite acquisition.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., TV on the left or laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, or dessert items with sauce THEN yes BECAUSE across observed left-attention meals with messier food conditions the user explicitly chose confirmation, consistent with wanting extra verification when bite acquisition may be less certain or more disruptive. IF dining context is quiet, social/front-attention, or front-TV AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special sauce/dip condition THEN no BECAUSE observed non-messy meals support a lower-interaction workflow when extra confirmation is not needed.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user's lack of trunk control prevents leaning forward comfortably, and all observed selections are consistent with this physical constraint.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer in any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE this parameter only applies to outside-mouth transfer, which is incompatible with the user's physical limitations and unsupported by observed selections.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., TV on the left or laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, or dessert items with sauce THEN speech + LED BECAUSE in observed left-attention meals the user chose a multimodal cue, likely preferring a more salient signal while attention is divided. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front (e.g., watching TV in front) AND affective state is neutral AND food has no sauce or dip THEN LED BECAUSE repeated front-TV meals used the simpler visual cue.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF any dining context where button use would be required AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. IF any observed dining context AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user consistently chose low-effort automatic initiation across social, front-attention, and left-attention meals. This indicates a stable preference for minimizing effort over more explicit initiation methods.

IF any dining context where button use would be required AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. IF any observed dining context AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user consistently chose low-effort automatic initiation across meals, indicating a stable preference for minimizing effort.

IF any dining context where button use would be required AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. IF any observed dining context AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user consistently chose low-effort automatic initiation across meals, indicating a stable preference for minimizing effort.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., TV on the left or laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, or dessert items with sauce THEN speech + LED BECAUSE in observed left-attention meals the user chose a multimodal completion cue, likely preferring a more noticeable signal while attention is divided. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front (e.g., watching TV in front) AND affective state is neutral AND food has no sauce or dip THEN LED BECAUSE repeated front-TV meals used the simpler visual completion cue.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND feeding transfer occurs THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently selected the reliable low-effort completion method, and button use is incompatible with severe upper-limb paralysis.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF any dining context where button use would be required AND any affective state AND drinking transfer occurs THEN perception BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. IF dining context is quiet, front-attention, or left-attention AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN low-effort non-button methods are preferred, with evidence split between autocontinue and perception. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front (e.g., watching TV in front) AND affective state is neutral AND food has no sauce or dip THEN perception BECAUSE repeated front-TV meals used perception rather than autocontinue.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF any dining context where button use would be required AND any affective state AND mouth wiping occurs THEN perception BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. IF dining context is quiet, front-attention, or left-attention AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN low-effort non-button methods are preferred, with evidence split between autocontinue and perception. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front (e.g., watching TV in front) AND affective state is neutral AND food has no sauce or dip THEN perception BECAUSE repeated front-TV meals used perception rather than autocontinue.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to external media or work (e.g., TV on the left, laptop on the left, or TV in front) AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN yes BECAUSE across all observed media-attention meals the user chose retraction, consistent with prioritizing reduced visual obstruction when attending to a screen. IF dining context is social with a person in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special sauce/dip condition THEN no BECAUSE the user accepted less interruption and shorter meal time in that meal.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food has no sauce or dip available AND any dining context AND any affective state THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not relevant. IF food includes sauce or dip AND dining context is social with a person in front AND affective state is neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user chose to avoid dipping in that meal, consistent with minimizing mess in social settings. IF food includes sauce or dip AND dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., TV on the left or laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE across observed left-attention meals the user preferred some dipping but not heavy dipping.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person in front OR includes a visual focus in front (e.g., watching TV in front) AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special sauce/dip condition THEN 10 sec BECAUSE repeated forward-attention meals used the shortest wait, indicating preference for quick progression in these contexts. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., TV on the left or laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, or dessert items with sauce THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE across observed left-attention meals the user explicitly chose the longest wait, indicating a strong preference for ample time to intervene when attention is divided and the food may be messier.
